description_html:
Are you passionate about hunting for new business and driving growth within the UKI enterprise market? 

Kahoot! is looking for a Senior Account Executive, New Business to join our North Sales team (UKI, Nordic & Benelux) with a specific focus on the UKI region. 

Join Kahoot!, one of the world's fastest-growing SaaS companies, and be part of our dynamic growth journey! In this role, you won't just sell software; you will act as a trusted advisor to new Enterprise customers (+2,000 employees), identifying opportunities to solve business challenges and ensuring our solutions deliver high-value impact within their organizations.You will be the architect of our future growth, spearheading activities to acquire new business through a structured, value-based approach. If you thrive in a global environment, embrace the challenge of the "hunt," and are driven to succeed, this is an exceptional opportunity to excel within our EMEA Sales team.

This position is based in our office centrally located in Copenhagen, Denmark or Oslo, Norway.

The role

Your main responsibility will be to strategically expand your portfolio of new clients across targeted industries in UKI. You will be a key member of the team, responsible for the entire sales cycle from initial prospecting to closing deals with some of the world's leading companies.In this role, you will report to the Regional Sales Manager.



Responsibilities

  • Proactively build and grow a strong pipeline of large-account (2,000+ employees) opportunities through targeted new logo acquisition and driving expansion opportunity within your Book of Business (UKI based companies)
  • Lead a solution and value-based sales process from initial prospecting and pipeline building to closing
  • Be the subject matter expert in presenting the value and drive the sales process across the Kahoot! suite
  • Work closely with BDR, Solution Engineering, Customer Success and Channel teams to tailor solutions and ensure exceptional customer experiences throughout the sales process
  • Effectively manage and communicate with senior key stakeholders to align Kahoot!'s solutions with their core business goals
  • Maintain a high standard of pipeline coverage (4x+ coverage ratio), forecasting rigor and CRM hygiene: weekly commit/best‑case, risk/upsides, crisp close plans, and maintaining an accurate hygiene
  • Consistently exceed quarterly revenue targets through disciplined execution
  • Measure success through disciplined account execution, enterprise deal velocity, deal quality (average deal size, conversion rates), sales cycle management, and forecast accuracy


Requirements

  • BA/BS degree or equivalent
  • Proven enterprise SaaS track record, preferable from the UK market, with ≥100% quota attainment and multiple multi‑threaded deals
  • End‑to‑end ownership of 3-9 month expansion deal cycles, navigating Procurement, Legal, and InfoSec, with disciplined use of MEDDICC to control the sales process
  • Proven success in managing and growing a Book of Business, demonstrated success in generating and closing new enterprise logos and securing expansions
  • Rigid forecasting rigor and CRM hygiene (weekly commit/best‑case, risks/upsides, accurate product mix) with hands‑on. proficiency, preferable in HubSpot
  • Strong business acumen and ability to navigate large organisations, engaging decision-makers and key stakeholders
  • Comfortable in a result-oriented environment where things are moving fast
  • A positive, proactive attitude and a growth-oriented minds
  • Can relate to our Commercial Mantras: Connecting, Driving, Impacting
  • Fluent in English, both verbal and written.

At Kahoot!, we know that the best ideas come from working closely together. That's why we're working from the office four days a week—collaborating, sharing ideas, and building meaningful relationships while working on our exciting and ambitious goals. If you thrive in a dynamic, creative environment where teamwork and strong connections are at the heart of everything we do, this is the place for you.

Benefits

  • A competitive compensation package
  • Paid maternity and paternity leave
  • Home broadband allowance
  • Mobile subscription
  • Lunch contribution
  • Flexible working
  • Buddy program 
  • Social and company events (virtual and in person)
  • A diverse, friendly and international environment

About Kahoot!

The Kahoot! Group is on a mission to make learning awesome! We want to empower everyone, including children, students, and employees to unlock their full learning potential. Our Kahoot! learning platform makes it easy for any individual or corporation to create, share, and host learning sessions that drive compelling engagement.

Launched in 2013, Kahoot!'s vision is to build the leading learning platform in the world. Since launch, Kahoot! has hosted hundreds of millions of learning sessions with more than 10 billion participants (non-unique) in more than 200 countries and regions. The Kahoot! Group includes Clever, the leading US K-12 EdTech learning platform, together with the learning apps DragonBox, Poio, Drops, Actimo, Motimate, and Whiteboard.fi.

The Kahoot! Group is headquartered in Oslo, Norway, with offices in the US, the UK, France, Finland, Estonia, Denmark, Spain and Poland. To learn more, visit us at kahoot.com, and let's play!

Our K!rew

At the Kahoot! Group we champion a positive culture of collaborative learning and innovation. With a global team of more than 500 employees representing over 50 different nationalities, we're a diverse and fun bunch of people! We work hard and celebrate our wins, tackle challenges with original ideas and learn something new every day. Every day we inspire our customers into new opportunities, leveraging our platforms and driving world-class employee engagement, learning and communication. We are driven by our mission and guided by our values: playful, curious and inclusive.  

Does this sound like the right right for you? Join us in making learning awesome!

descriptionhtml:
Glovo is part of the Delivery Hero Group, the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience—fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide. Headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index.

Job Description

The Mission

The XL team manages Glovo Spain's largest accounts. As a Senior Account Manager, you own a portfolio of these accounts end-to-end: the relationship, the commercial agenda, and the day-to-day execution.

This is a commercial role at its core. You will be managing senior relationships with major national brands, running structured business reviews, identifying growth opportunities, and executing the levers that move performance. You will also be the internal coordinator when things need to get done — working across Finance, Operations, Ads, and Marketing to make sure what is agreed actually happens.

The role rewards people who combine strong commercial judgment with the discipline to stay on top of the detail. Both matter here.

What You Will Do

  • Own the relationship: You are the primary point of contact for your portfolio. You build trust with your partners' key stakeholders, understand their business priorities, and manage the relationship proactively — not just at review time.
  • Lead business reviews: You prepare and deliver MBRs and QBRs end-to-end: clear narrative, data-backed, with a concrete action plan. You run the conversation, not just the presentation.
  • Monitor and act on performance: You track how your accounts are performing across key metrics, and bring a point of view and a recommendation when something needs to change.
  • Execute the commercial agenda: You manage promotional activations, advertising commitments, and marketing initiatives. You identify opportunities to grow the partnership and pursue them at the right moment with a well-framed pitch.
  • Coordinate internally: You are the person who makes sure commitments to partners get delivered. That means working cross-functionally to resolve issues — billing, operations, campaign execution, etc. — and keeping partners informed throughout.
  • Build commercial proposals: You prepare structured materials to support growth conversations: account plans, opportunity analysis, investment cases. You think ahead and bring that thinking to your partners.

What Makes This Role Distinctive

  • Small portfolio, high stakes: You manage a small number of major national accounts. The relationships are deep, the conversations are substantive, and your impact is directly visible.
  • Real commercial scope: This is not a maintenance role. You will be involved in promotional strategy, investment discussions, and growth planning with some of the most important restaurant brands in Spain.
  • High standards, fast feedback: as any team at Glovo, the XL team operates with a very high bar for preparation and follow-through. You will be expected to meet it — and you will find the environment sharper for it.

Qualifications

What You Bring

  • Commercial acumen: You know how to build a case, read a room, and move a conversation forward. You are comfortable in senior commercial discussions and have a track record of driving outcomes in complex, high-value relationships.
  • Strong communication: You present with confidence and structure - write with clarity, and adapt your style to the audience. You are direct without being blunt, and persuasive without overselling.
  • Structured and reliable: You manage a demanding workload without losing track of the detail. Partners and internal teams know that when you own something, it gets done.
  • Comfortable with data: You can navigate dashboards and spreadsheets, extract what matters, and use it to support a clear argument. You do not need to be a data specialist — you need to be someone who uses data well in a commercial context.
  • +4 years in senior account management or commercial client-facing roles: Background in food delivery, marketplace platforms, FMCG, or high-growth tech is a strong plus. What matters is that you have managed significant accounts and driven commercial outcomes independently.
  • Full professional proficiency in English, both written and verbal.

Individuals representing diverse profiles, and abilities, encompassing various genders, ethnicities, and backgrounds, are less likely to apply for this role if they do not possess solid experience in 100% of these areas. Even if it seems you don't meet our musts don't let it stop you, we are all about finding the best talent out there! Skills can be learned, and embracing diversity is invaluable.

What We Offer

  • A portfolio of Spain's most important QSR accounts, including some of the most recognised restaurant brands in the country.
  • A fast-paced environment where commercial initiative and independent judgment are valued.
  • Hybrid working model based in Madrid.
  • Competitive package with variable component tied to portfolio performance.
  • Real exposure to senior commercial decisions and room to grow.

We Believe Driven Talent Deserves

  • 🌟 An enticing equity plan that lets you own a piece of the action.
  • 💪 Top-notch private health insurance to keep you at your peak.
  • 🍔 Monthly Glovo credit to satisfy your cravings!
  • 💳 Discounts on transportation, food, and even kindergarten expenses.
  • 🏊 Discounted gym memberships to keep you energized.
  • 🏖️ Extra time off, the freedom to work from home two days a week, and the opportunity to work from anywhere for up to three weeks a year!
  • 👪 Enhanced parental leave.
  • 🧠 Online therapy and wellbeing benefits to ensure your mental well-being.

Here at Glovo, we thrive on diversity, we believe it enhances our teams, products, and culture. We know that the best ideas come from a mashup of brilliant diverse minds. This is why we are committed to providing equal opportunities to talent from all backgrounds - all genders, racial/diverse backgrounds, abilities, ages, sexual orientations and all other unique characteristics that make you YOU. We will encourage you to bring your authentic self to work, fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels heard.

Feel free to note your pronouns in your application (e.g., she/her/hers, he/him/his, they/them/theirs, etc).

So, ready to take the wheel and make this the ride of your life?

Delve into our culture by taking a peek at our Instagram and check out our Linkedin and website!

Additional Information

Here at Glovo, we thrive on diversity, we believe it enhances our teams, products, and culture. We know that the best ideas come from a mashup of brilliant diverse minds. This is why we are committed to providing equal opportunities to talent from all backgrounds – all genders, racial/diverse backgrounds, abilities, ages, sexual orientations and all other unique characteristics that make you YOU. We will encourage you to bring your authentic self to work, fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels heard.

Feel free to note your pronouns in your application (e.g., she/her/hers, he/him/his, they/them/theirs, etc).

So, ready to take the wheel and make this the ride of your life?

Delve into our culture by taking a peek at our Instagram and check out our Linkedin and website!

description_html:
Glovo is part of the Delivery Hero Group, the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience—fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide. Headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index.

Job Description

The Mission

The XL team manages Glovo Spain's largest accounts. As a Senior Account Manager, you own a portfolio of these accounts end-to-end: the relationship, the commercial agenda, and the day-to-day execution.

This is a commercial role at its core. You will be managing senior relationships with major national brands, running structured business reviews, identifying growth opportunities, and executing the levers that move performance. You will also be the internal coordinator when things need to get done — working across Finance, Operations, Ads, and Marketing to make sure what is agreed actually happens.

The role rewards people who combine strong commercial judgment with the discipline to stay on top of the detail. Both matter here.

What You Will Do

  • Own the relationship: You are the primary point of contact for your portfolio. You build trust with your partners' key stakeholders, understand their business priorities, and manage the relationship proactively — not just at review time.
  • Lead business reviews: You prepare and deliver MBRs and QBRs end-to-end: clear narrative, data-backed, with a concrete action plan. You run the conversation, not just the presentation.
  • Monitor and act on performance: You track how your accounts are performing across key metrics, and bring a point of view and a recommendation when something needs to change.
  • Execute the commercial agenda: You manage promotional activations, advertising commitments, and marketing initiatives. You identify opportunities to grow the partnership and pursue them at the right moment with a well-framed pitch.
  • Coordinate internally: You are the person who makes sure commitments to partners get delivered. That means working cross-functionally to resolve issues — billing, operations, campaign execution, etc. — and keeping partners informed throughout.
  • Build commercial proposals: You prepare structured materials to support growth conversations: account plans, opportunity analysis, investment cases. You think ahead and bring that thinking to your partners.

What Makes This Role Distinctive

  • Small portfolio, high stakes: You manage a small number of major national accounts. The relationships are deep, the conversations are substantive, and your impact is directly visible.
  • Real commercial scope: This is not a maintenance role. You will be involved in promotional strategy, investment discussions, and growth planning with some of the most important restaurant brands in Spain.
  • High standards, fast feedback: as any team at Glovo, the XL team operates with a very high bar for preparation and follow-through. You will be expected to meet it — and you will find the environment sharper for it.

Qualifications

What You Bring

  • Commercial acumen: You know how to build a case, read a room, and move a conversation forward. You are comfortable in senior commercial discussions and have a track record of driving outcomes in complex, high-value relationships.
  • Strong communication: You present with confidence and structure - write with clarity, and adapt your style to the audience. You are direct without being blunt, and persuasive without overselling.
  • Structured and reliable: You manage a demanding workload without losing track of the detail. Partners and internal teams know that when you own something, it gets done.
  • Comfortable with data: You can navigate dashboards and spreadsheets, extract what matters, and use it to support a clear argument. You do not need to be a data specialist — you need to be someone who uses data well in a commercial context.
  • +4 years in senior account management or commercial client-facing roles: Background in food delivery, marketplace platforms, FMCG, or high-growth tech is a strong plus. What matters is that you have managed significant accounts and driven commercial outcomes independently.
  • Full professional proficiency in English, both written and verbal.

Individuals representing diverse profiles, and abilities, encompassing various genders, ethnicities, and backgrounds, are less likely to apply for this role if they do not possess solid experience in 100% of these areas. Even if it seems you don't meet our musts don't let it stop you, we are all about finding the best talent out there! Skills can be learned, and embracing diversity is invaluable.

What We Offer

  • A portfolio of Spain's most important QSR accounts, including some of the most recognised restaurant brands in the country.
  • A fast-paced environment where commercial initiative and independent judgment are valued.
  • Hybrid working model based in Madrid.
  • Competitive package with variable component tied to portfolio performance.
  • Real exposure to senior commercial decisions and room to grow.

We Believe Driven Talent Deserves

  • 🌟 An enticing equity plan that lets you own a piece of the action.
  • 💪 Top-notch private health insurance to keep you at your peak.
  • 🍔 Monthly Glovo credit to satisfy your cravings!
  • 💳 Discounts on transportation, food, and even kindergarten expenses.
  • 🏊 Discounted gym memberships to keep you energized.
  • 🏖️ Extra time off, the freedom to work from home two days a week, and the opportunity to work from anywhere for up to three weeks a year!
  • 👪 Enhanced parental leave.
  • 🧠 Online therapy and wellbeing benefits to ensure your mental well-being.

Here at Glovo, we thrive on diversity, we believe it enhances our teams, products, and culture. We know that the best ideas come from a mashup of brilliant diverse minds. This is why we are committed to providing equal opportunities to talent from all backgrounds - all genders, racial/diverse backgrounds, abilities, ages, sexual orientations and all other unique characteristics that make you YOU. We will encourage you to bring your authentic self to work, fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels heard.

Feel free to note your pronouns in your application (e.g., she/her/hers, he/him/his, they/them/theirs, etc).

So, ready to take the wheel and make this the ride of your life?

Delve into our culture by taking a peek at our Instagram and check out our Linkedin and website!

title: (Senior) Account Executive
descriptionhtml:
Join Our Dynamic Sales Team and Become a Pioneer in the semiconductor industry!

Are you a tenacious communicator with a passion for managing the full sales cycle and building meaningful relationships with electronics companies from around the world? If so, then the (Senior) Account Executive position at Smith is the perfect opportunity for you to elevate your career and become a driving force in our ever-evolving industry.

Smith is the leading independent distributor of electronic components and ranks number seven among all global distributors. In 19 cities around the world, our legion of employees communicates in 50 different languages and dialects and buys and sells components 24 hours per day, with global sales of more than $12 billion over the last five years. We’re always moving; helping manufacturers navigate market shifts, customizing supply chain solutions, testing components using cutting-edge technology, and more.

We are looking for a new team member with a proven sales track record in the semiconductor industry (independent distribution) to join our team in Amsterdam. You will use your knowledge and experience of the industry to expand Smith’s customer portfolio, generating high-revenue projects.

  • Job-type: Full-time
  • Start date: ASAP
  • Location: Amsterdam (hybrid)

Key Responsibilities

  • Sales Strategy: Develop and implement effective sales strategies to achieve sales targets;
  • Customer Relationship Management: Build and maintain strong relationships with key accounts and decision-makers;
  • Business Development: Identify new business opportunities and expand the customer base in EMEA with your existing customer relationship;
  • Value-Added Services: Effectively communicate the benefits of Smith's value-added services, such as cost savings, excess inventory management, and Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI);
  • Market Analysis: Stay informed about industry trends, competitive landscapes, and customer needs;
  • Cross-Functional Collaboration: Collaborate with other departments, such as marketing, operations, and finance, to ensure seamless execution of sales strategies;
  • Team Leadership and Mentorship: Provide guidance and support to junior account representatives.

Key Requirements

  • Must have at least 6+ years of relevant experience within the semiconductor industry (independent distribution);
  • Fluent in English; fluency in another European language is an advantage;
  • Drive in a fast-paced working environment and work well independently with little managerial involvement;
  • Service and results oriented;
  • Commitment to customer satisfaction;
  • Ability to maintain customer confidentiality and manage proprietary data;
  • Ability to overcome customer objections.

Benefits

  • Commuting allowance to facilitate your journey to work.
  • 25 annual paid leave to take a moment to unwind and recharge.
  • Wellness reimbursement plan to support your fitness and wellness goals
  • Comprehensive pension plan
  • Disability and accident insurance benefits
  • Access to an exclusive learning platform where you can expand your knowledge, refine your skills
  • Regular team and sport activities and lunches, fostering strong bonds and a positive work environment

We are an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer.

description_text: Customer Success Manager – Pivot – Permanent contract in Paris Pivot Customer Success Manager Permanent contract Paris No remote work Salary: Not specified Apply Save 14 days ago Share Copy link Share on Facebook Share on X Share on LinkedIn Pivot Interested in this job? Apply Save Questions and answers about the job Is it mandatory to attach a resume? Yes, you are required to attach your resume to apply for this job. Does this position allow remote work? Type of contract for this position: Permanent contract Is a cover letter mandatory to apply for this position? A cover letter is optional to apply for this position. The position Job description About us Today, our market (upper mid-market & enterprise companies, from 500 employees to fortune 500 companies) is vastly underserved when it comes to their procurement processes. These companies struggle with outdated and old fashioned tools, dragged down by decades of tech legacy that create more problems than they solve in the day-to-day work of their employees. Pivot is solving this by building an AI Operating System for Procurement. Our goal is to streamline purchase processes for employees while giving finance teams the right level of control, visibility, and reporting. Through automations, integrations, and AI, we simplify workflows across finance teams, freeing them up to focus on more strategic work. The journey started mid-2023 with Marc-Antoine, Romain, and Estelle, former C-levels & tech lead from fintech unicorns (Qonto, Swile, Highland). Since then, we have raised 2 funding rounds (€5M pre-seed and €20M Series A) with tier-1 investors, built a team of 65+ people, and grown to 100+ customers across 25+ countries (EMEA and US). We are now preparing our next acceleration phase, and are looking for smart, execution-driven people with a strong focus on excellence and a desire to scale as a team. Your role Pivot is seeking a highly motivated and experienced Customer Operations Success Manager to join our Operations team. This role is crucial in ensuring our clients receive outstanding support and an exceptional customer experience. You will be the first line of defense for any kind of client issues or requests and will play a key role in evolving our Operations processes and tools. Working hand-in-hand with the Customer Success Lead and the Customer Operations managers, you’ll be fundamental in ensuring customer satisfaction, our north star KPI at Pivot. Customer Care excellence Provide top-notch care to clients, addressing and resolving their issues efficiently and effectively in collaboration with the tech team. Manage support tickets from creation to closure, ensuring timely and accurate resolutions. Investigate and escalate technical issues to the product and tech teams, relying on your in-depth product knowledge to analyze customer requests. This is not basic customer support. Draw analyses from what you see, identify root causes and define action plan to improve our solution Client Setup and Evolution Update existing clients setups based on our client feedback and needs, including workflows, intake forms etc. Ensure all client interactions are documented and follow up on client requests promptly Technical project management: Work closely with product, tech, and integrations teams to relay client feedback and drive product improvements. Follow up on the development of new features and improvements, participating in product spec reviews and challenges. Customer Success Client satisfaction Ensure client satisfaction and follow-up closely on your accounts (i.e., QBR, success metrics) Upsell Identify upsell opportunities (in collaboration with sales team) Renewals and anti-churn Identify potential risks and counter-measure to avoid churns Ensure renewals of contracts (in collaboration with sales team) Tooling and documentation Tools optimization and implementation Evaluate and implement new customer support tools, including communication channels and ticketing systems. Conduct benchmarking and select the best solutions to enhance our support infrastructure and clients delivery tracking methods Documentation and process Set-up new documentation and processes to support both Customer Support and Success improvements Your Mindset A solver who sees every challenge as an opportunity. A client-first approach, ready to go above and beyond client expectations A growth-minded individual eager to scale with a dynamic company. Requirements You have at least 3 years of experience in customer success, customer support, or a related field within a SaaS or software environment. Excellent problem-solving abilities and technical proficiency to troubleshoot product-related issues. You know how to talk to clients, delivering relevant answers and reinsuring them You have strong project management skills and the ability to juggle multiple priorities. You have a proven track record of managing support operations and improving customer service processes and tools. You have experience in a startup environment, building support systems and processes from scratch. You are fluent in English. The position Job description About us Today, our market (upper mid-market & enterprise companies, from 500 employees to fortune 500 companies) is vastly underserved when it comes to their procurement processes. These companies struggle with outdated and old fashioned tools, dragged down by decades of tech legacy that create more problems than they solve in the day-to-day work of their employees. Pivot is solving this by building an AI Operating System for Procurement. Our goal is to streamline purchase processes for employees while giving finance teams the right level of control, visibility, and reporting. Through automations, integrations, and AI, we simplify workflows across finance teams, freeing them up to focus on more strategic work. The journey started mid-2023 with Marc-Antoine, Romain, and Estelle, former C-levels & tech lead from fintech unicorns (Qonto, Swile, Highland). Since then, we have raised 2 funding rounds (€5M pre-seed and €20M Series A) with tier-1 investors, built a team of 65+ people, and grown to 100+ customers across 25+ countries (EMEA and US). We are now preparing our next acceleration phase, and are looking for smart, execution-driven people with a strong focus on excellence and a desire to scale as a team. Your role Pivot is seeking a highly motivated and experienced Customer Operations Success Manager to join our Operations team. This role is crucial in ensuring our clients receive outstanding support and an exceptional customer experience. You will be the first line of defense for any kind of client issues or requests and will play a key role in evolving our Operations processes and tools. Working hand-in-hand with the Customer Success Lead and the Customer Operations managers, you’ll be fundamental in ensuring customer satisfaction, our north star KPI at Pivot. Customer Care excellence Provide top-notch care to clients, addressing and resolving their issues efficiently and effectively in collaboration with the tech team. Manage support tickets from creation to closure, ensuring timely and accurate resolutions. Investigate and escalate technical issues to the product and tech teams, relying on your in-depth product knowledge to analyze customer requests. This is not basic customer support. Draw analyses from what you see, identify root causes and define action plan to improve our solution Client Setup and Evolution Update existing clients setups based on our client feedback and needs, including workflows, intake forms etc. Ensure all client interactions are documented and follow up on client requests promptly Technical project management: Work closely with product, tech, and integrations teams to relay client feedback and drive product improvements. Follow up on the development of new features and improvements, participating in product spec reviews and challenges. Customer Success Client satisfaction Ensure client satisfaction and follow-up closely on your accounts (i.e., QBR, success metrics) Upsell Identify upsell opportunities (in collaboration with sales team) Renewals and anti-churn Identify potential risks and counter-measure to avoid churns Ensure renewals of contracts (in collaboration with sales team) Tooling and documentation Tools optimization and implementation Evaluate and implement new customer support tools, including communication channels and ticketing systems. Conduct benchmarking and select the best solutions to enhance our support infrastructure and clients delivery tracking methods Documentation and process Set-up new documentation and processes to support both Customer Support and Success improvements Your Mindset A solver who sees every challenge as an opportunity. A client-first approach, ready to go above and beyond client expectations A growth-minded individual eager to scale with a dynamic company. Requirements You have at least 3 years of experience in customer success, customer support, or a related field within a SaaS or software environment. Excellent problem-solving abilities and technical proficiency to troubleshoot product-related issues. You know how to talk to clients, delivering relevant answers and reinsuring them You have strong project management skills and the ability to juggle multiple priorities. You have a proven track record of managing support operations and improving customer service processes and tools. You have experience in a startup environment, building support systems and processes from scratch. You are fluent in English. You are proactive, able to prioritize, and willing to thrive in our rapidly growing startup environment and contribute energetically to our collective success. You pay close attention to details, an excellent listener, and a valuable team member. Le poste Descriptif du poste Reporting directly to our co-founder and sales director, Mathieu, your role will be to develop strong relationships and generate growth with around ten of our client accounts within your scope of action (North America & EMEA) alongside our 4 AE. You will be responsible for: Establishing a sales and loyalty strategy for each key account customer, Meeting and getting to know each key contact within our key account customers, Understanding and analyzing each customer’s needs and responding with a tailored solution , Building customer loyalty and ensuring customer satisfaction , Identifying opportunities and levers for developing the solution within the account (up-sell & cross-sell), Conducting sales negotiations and anticipating each contract renewal, Contributing to the animation of our customer community through partnerships and events. As a true leader, you will ensure effective coordination between the Customer Success, Sales & the marketing teams. 🔎 KPIs: Grow each customer account. Increase the Net New ARR of the portfolio in line with your target plan. Secure all contract renewals as part of multi-year commitments. Profil recherché You have already worked in a KAM position in the HRIS ecosystem for 4-5 years for huge groups and on a BtoB market . Your unfailing optimism and taste for challenge will be your best allies, You are responsive and organized, and you are not afraid of interacting with customers, You are fluent/native in French and English . Chez 365Talents, nous sommes convaincus que notre monde ne cessera jamais d’évoluer, et qu’il faudra forcément s’adapter et adapter nos compétences. C’est pourquoi de nombreux grands groupes internationaux font appel à 365Talents pour préparer leurs équipes à l’avenir et devenir des Organisations Basées sur les Compétences (SBO). Grâce à son moteur unique de Skills Intelligence basé sur l’IA, notre plateforme rend la gestion des compétences, de la mobilité et des carrières intuitive et accessible pour vos employés, managers et équipes RH. Notre plateforme utilise le Machine Learning et de puissants algorithmes pour détecter les compétences et expertises des collaborateurs et les associer à des opportunités internes personnalisées : emplois, projets, formations… Vous avancez ainsi en toute confiance, en ayant les bonnes personnes avec les bonnes compétences. La plateforme s’intègre avec tous les outils RH (HCM, LMS) et Digital Workplace. title: Customer Success Manager - Pivot - Plný úväzok v meste Paris
description_text: Customer Success Manager - Pivot - Plný úväzok v meste Paris Pivot Customer Success Manager Plný úväzok Paris Žiadna práca na diaľku Plat: Neuvedené Reagovať na ponuku Uložit pred 14 dňami Zdieľať Kopírovať odkaz Zdieľať na Facebooku Zdieľať na X Zdieľať na LinkedIn Pivot Máte záujem o túto ponuku? Reagovať na ponuku Uložit Otázky a odpovede ohľadne ponuky Je zaslanie životopisu povinné? Pri reagovaní na túto ponuku je potrebné pripojiť životopis. Je pri tejto pozícii možná práca na diaľku? Táto pozícia neponúka možnosť práce na diaľku. O akú formu spolupráce ide? Zmluva pre túto pozíciu je : Plný úväzok. Je motivačný list pri reagovaní na túto ponuku povinný? Pripojenie motivačného listu pri reagovaní na túto ponuku je voliteľné. Pozícia Popis pracovnej ponuky About us Today, our market (upper mid-market & enterprise companies, from 500 employees to fortune 500 companies) is vastly underserved when it comes to their procurement processes. These companies struggle with outdated and old fashioned tools, dragged down by decades of tech legacy that create more problems than they solve in the day-to-day work of their employees. Pivot is solving this by building an AI Operating System for Procurement. Our goal is to streamline purchase processes for employees while giving finance teams the right level of control, visibility, and reporting. Through automations, integrations, and AI, we simplify workflows across finance teams, freeing them up to focus on more strategic work. The journey started mid-2023 with Marc-Antoine, Romain, and Estelle, former C-levels & tech lead from fintech unicorns (Qonto, Swile, Highland). Since then, we have raised 2 funding rounds (€5M pre-seed and €20M Series A) with tier-1 investors, built a team of 65+ people, and grown to 100+ customers across 25+ countries (EMEA and US). We are now preparing our next acceleration phase, and are looking for smart, execution-driven people with a strong focus on excellence and a desire to scale as a team. Your role Pivot is seeking a highly motivated and experienced Customer Operations Success Manager to join our Operations team. This role is crucial in ensuring our clients receive outstanding support and an exceptional customer experience. You will be the first line of defense for any kind of client issues or requests and will play a key role in evolving our Operations processes and tools. Working hand-in-hand with the Customer Success Lead and the Customer Operations managers, you’ll be fundamental in ensuring customer satisfaction, our north star KPI at Pivot. Customer Care excellence Provide top-notch care to clients, addressing and resolving their issues efficiently and effectively in collaboration with the tech team. Manage support tickets from creation to closure, ensuring timely and accurate resolutions. Investigate and escalate technical issues to the product and tech teams, relying on your in-depth product knowledge to analyze customer requests. This is not basic customer support. Draw analyses from what you see, identify root causes and define action plan to improve our solution Client Setup and Evolution Update existing clients setups based on our client feedback and needs, including workflows, intake forms etc. Ensure all client interactions are documented and follow up on client requests promptly Technical project management: Work closely with product, tech, and integrations teams to relay client feedback and drive product improvements. Follow up on the development of new features and improvements, participating in product spec reviews and challenges. Customer Success Client satisfaction Ensure client satisfaction and follow-up closely on your accounts (i.e., QBR, success metrics) Upsell Identify upsell opportunities (in collaboration with sales team) Renewals and anti-churn Identify potential risks and counter-measure to avoid churns Ensure renewals of contracts (in collaboration with sales team) Tooling and documentation Tools optimization and implementation Evaluate and implement new customer support tools, including communication channels and ticketing systems. Conduct benchmarking and select the best solutions to enhance our support infrastructure and clients delivery tracking methods Documentation and process Set-up new documentation and processes to support both Customer Support and Success improvements Your Mindset A solver who sees every challenge as an opportunity. A client-first approach, ready to go above and beyond client expectations A growth-minded individual eager to scale with a dynamic company. Requirements You have at least 3 years of experience in customer success, customer support, or a related field within a SaaS or software environment. Excellent problem-solving abilities and technical proficiency to troubleshoot product-related issues. You know how to talk to clients, delivering relevant answers and reinsuring them You have strong project management skills and the ability to juggle multiple priorities. You have a proven track record of managing support operations and improving customer service processes and tools. You have experience in a startup environment, building support systems and processes from scratch. You are fluent in English. You are proactive, able to prioritize, and willing to thrive in our rapidly growing startup environment and contribute energetically to our collective success. You pay close attention to details, an excellent listener, and a valuable team member. What you will get A competitive salary package plus equity (BSPCE) RTT Meal vouchers Swile 1 days of remote work per week An office at Morning Bourse in Paris The richness of a multicultural and international team (more than 15 nationalities). A unique chance to grow with a fast-scaling innovator Chcete sa dozvedieť viac? Preskúmajte spoločnosť Sledujte ich! Pivot is a B2B SaaS that is revolutionizing procurement management for mid-market companies (300 to 5,000 employees). Our mission: to become the leader in the EMEA market. To achieve this, our teams are building a smart and intuitive product designed to streamline purchasing processes while ensuring effective control and reporting. Founded in 2023 by former leaders of Qonto, Swile and Nuxeo, we have already raised €25M and deployed our product to clients in 10 countries.
